title: socks5_helper IPv6 rejection is a maintainer trap
description:
socks5_helper.rs lines 52-68 handle only ATYP=1 (IPv4) and ATYP=3 (domain), deliberately panicking on ATYP=4 (IPv6). The panic happens inside a spawned task, silently terminating the test proxy without signaling the test.

Risk: When IPv6 support is added (e.g., parse_relay_addr handles ::1), a contributor might not realize socks5_helper.rs needs updating. The test will silently fail on CI because the proxy panics before the CONNECT is signaled to the test.

Mitigations:
1. Add a doc comment with explicit IPv6 upgrade path: 'When IPv6 is needed, add ATYP=4 handling here (16 bytes address + 2 port)'
2. Consider: spawn socks5_helper as an external standalone binary so it can panic safely (currently in-process)
3. Or: assert at test runtime that parse_relay_addr never produces IPv6 output
